title: Thelypteris longicaulis
taxonomy: Thelypteris longicaulis is classified within the family Thelypteridaceae and is a species of fern. It belongs to the genus Thelypteris, which is part of the division Pteridophyta. The family Thelypteridaceae is a group of ferns that are commonly found in tropical and subtropical regions. The classification of Thelypteris longicaulis is further divided into the kingdom Plantae and the phylum Tracheophyta. Thelypteris longicaulis is a type of deciduous fern that is native to Central and South America.

intro: Thelypteris longicaulis, also known as the long-stalked lady fern, is a species of flowering plant in the family Thelypteridaceae. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, making it a promising treatment for various ailments such as wounds, fever, and digestive issues. Bioactive constituents of Thelypteris longicaulis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made from this plant include teas, infusions, and extracts, which are often used to treat respiratory issues, fever, and skin conditions.
